beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Fresh start to the week with full energy; morning routine before heading out on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Drive from home to workshop in Redmond.
beats.reasoning: Commute to workplace across town; budgeted 45 min for typical morning traffic on I-90.

beats.activity: Work on custom furniture order in the shop.
beats.reasoning: Focus time on carpentry before the midday break; USA-Wales match at 11am is not a priority for this agent.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a local café near the workshop.
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch nearby rather than heading downtown for the match; Ellie prefers close friends to crowds, so the FIFA bar scene doesn't appeal.

beats.activity: Return to shop and continue furniture work.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push on the custom order; sunny weather makes for good natural light in the workshop.

beats.activity: Drive from Redmond workshop back to Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: Early exit to beat rush hour; 40 min drive home on I-90.

beats.activity: Stop at a local bookstore to browse and pick up a new poetry collection.
beats.reasoning: Can't resist a bookstore; on the way home from Redmond, feeds her love of poetry and reading.
Final commute home; short drive from Lake Forest Park to Bellevue.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Home-cooked meal; time to relax after a productive workday.

diary: Started the day right with strong coffee and the kitchen quiet—these early mornings are mine before the world gets loud. The drive to Redmond was smooth, sunny pouring through the windshield. Got into the groove at the shop, working on this dining table commission with some tricky inlays. Broke for lunch at Redmond Town Center and just sat outside with a sandwich, watching people move past. Heard folks talking about some soccer match downtown, but the workshop called me back. Lost track of time doing detail work—that's when I know I'm in the right headspace. On the way home I swung by Third Place Books in Lake Forest Park, grabbed a new poetry collection I'd been eyeing. There's something about holding a new book, still crisp. Made pasta, ate alone, felt the day settle into my bones.
thought_process: The morning routine grounded me before a solid day of work. I wasn't tempted by the early-opening bars for the World Cup—that scene isn't really mine anymore, and I had meaningful work to do. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, which felt like a small luck. My mood stayed steady and focused through the furniture work; there's a meditation in precise joinery. Lunch was a deliberate pause—I needed it. The bookstore stop felt like a gift I gave myself, a small indulgence on the drive home. Poetry helps me process things. By dinner I was pleasantly tired, the kind of tired that means I used my hands and mind well.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
